batch_summary: Deed [316] — Warranty deed, Verona E. Black (widow) → Mary Rees Wilson (widow, Norman OK), June 8, 1965. Conveys 56.8 acres surface + ALL minerals, Lipscomb Norvell Survey A-20, Limestone County. This is the companion conveyance deed to the previously pulled [314] affidavit of title (same-day filing, LC Vol. 516). DISPOSED property — the Norvell Survey tract permanently left the family chain in 1965. Confirms the Wilson disposition referenced in ISSUE-43 item 9 was Norvell Survey, NOT Boyd Survey — no impact on ISSUE-01 Boyd Survey chain analysis. Documentary stamps ($4.95) indicate actual consideration of approximately $4,001–$4,500.

operations.description: Add deed [316] entry to Phase 2, after deed [314]
operations.content: 5. `1965-06-08_[316]_l` — Verona E. Black (widow) → Mary Rees Wilson (widow, Norman OK) (warranty deed, surface+minerals, Limestone) — 56.8 ac Lipscomb Norvell Survey, Abstract No. 20, Limestone County. Metes-and-bounds identical to [314] affidavit: beginning at T. W. Wade's SW corner in S line of 400-ac John Caruthers survey; S 430 vrs; E 745-8/10 vrs to Jones NE corner; N 430 vrs; W 745-8/10 vrs to POB. **Minerals: FULL CONVEYANCE** — "Grantor herein hereby conveys to grantee herein all of the oil, gas and other minerals in, to and under said above described land." No reservation. Consideration: $10.00 + OGVC; documentary stamps $4.95 (4×$1.10 + 1×$0.55; implied actual consideration ~$4,001–$4,500 per 1965 federal rate of $0.55/$500). General warranty. Acknowledged Jun 9, 1965 before Joe Schultz NP, Limestone County (same attorney firm as [314]: Schultz & Martin, Mexia TX). LC Pg. 307–308, Doc# 3775. Filed Jul 26, 1965 at 10:00 AM; recorded Jul 28, 1965 at 4:00 PM; John Kidd, County Clerk; Eleanor Popejoy, Deputy. **DISPOSED — property permanently left family chain.** Companion to [314] affidavit (Pg. 304, Doc# 3773, same-day filing). Completes Norvell Survey disposition chain: W.E. Black + Ida A. Black → JT Black (1943) → heirs → Verona E. Black (1959, [311]) → Mary Rees Wilson (1965, [316]).

operations.description: Add [316] to Phase 2 deed list in Phase Structure Overview table

operations.description: Add deed [316] row to Deed Register table, after [314]
operations.content: | [316] | Verona E. Black (widow) → Mary Rees Wilson (widow, Norman OK) | Limestone | ✓ | LC Vol. 516, Pg. 307–308, Doc# 3775. Filed Jul 26, 1965 at 10:00 AM; recorded Jul 28, 1965 at 4:00 PM; John Kidd, County Clerk; Eleanor Popejoy, Deputy; Limestone County. | 307–308 (2 pages) | 1965-06-08 | 1965-07-28 | **Warranty Deed** — DISPOSED PROPERTY (Norvell Survey). 56.8 ac Lipscomb Norvell Survey, Abstract No. 20, LC. Metes-and-bounds identical to [314] affidavit. Surface + ALL minerals conveyed (explicit: "Grantor herein hereby conveys to grantee herein all of the oil, gas and other minerals in, to and under said above described land"). Consideration: $10.00 + OGVC; documentary stamps $4.95 (4×$1.10 + 1×$0.55; implied actual consideration ~$4,001–$4,500 per 1965 federal stamp rate of $0.55/$500). General warranty deed — warrant and forever defend. Grantee residence: City of Norman, State of Oklahoma. Acknowledged Jun 9, 1965 before Joe Schultz NP, Limestone County (same attorney firm as [314]: Schultz & Martin, Mexia TX; Joe Schultz is the notary on both instruments). Companion to [314] affidavit (Pg. 304, Doc# 3773, same-day filing Jul 26, 1965). **Completes Norvell Survey disposition chain**: W.E. Black + Ida A. Black (pre-~1925) → JT Black (1943) → heirs by intestacy → Verona E. Black (1959, [311]) → Mary Rees Wilson (1965, [316]). Property permanently left family chain. ISSUE-43 item 9 (pg. 307 portion). Phase 2 |
